ending: Earth completes the corrupted-invader trial, receives two tier-one Nexus citizenship tokens, and avoids inheriting the invaders' displaced populations and energy burden. The victory is followed by a Great Rift and a Nether rescue offensive aimed at the subdued Nether King supporting the seventh cohort. Randidly, Karen's veterans, Alana, Hank, Helen, and other defenders eliminate the initial surface probes. The Nexus pinnacle figure then orders Earth protected, and a specialist response is due. Earth is spherical again, with a new zone, unstable borderlands, emerging bubble cities, and a runic tablet that can grant a formal Nexus name. Randidly refuses to claim the available token or control the naming contest. He plans to withdraw Karen and his organization from directing Earth, leaving Tatiana and Naffer to strengthen Karen through alliances. His departure for the Nexus must wait until he deals with the disgraced investigator abandoned on Earth and reclaims Yestix's memories from Ace and Rose. The Great Rift is contained but not resolved, Lyra's freedom remains uncertain, and Ace continues building a rival threat.
in_short: Randidly returns to Earth and a rapidly growing Karen, determined to prepare the planet without ruling it. His restraint contributes to the catastrophic stadium massacre, after which he gathers Earth's powers and coordinates a corrupted-invader trial secretly designed to expose his Nether. He remakes Ignition Essence as the Stillborn Phoenix, neutralizes the investigator's Aether Mines, and breaks a System limit on his body. Earth clears the trial, but a Great Rift opens and sends Nether forces to the surface. Randidly and Earth's defenders destroy the first incursion, while the Nexus's supreme power orders specialists to protect the planet. With Earth transformed and politically exposed, Randidly decides to leave governance to Tatiana, Naffer, and Earth's factions. Before departing for the Nexus, he intends to kill the stranded investigator and recover Yestix's stolen memories from Ace and Rose.

recaps.text: Previously: Randidly Ghosthound became one of Earth's strongest protectors, founded the moving city of Karen, and developed three major images while building an unusual command of both Aether and Nether. His path also drew him into Nexus military service and conflict with powerful families and System authorities. Nevaeh remained his soulbound companion and Aether specialist, while Tatiana managed Karen and allies such as Helen, Alana, and Hank defended Earth. Randidly's old friend Ace moved toward blood-based power and opposition. Several pieces of a fate set remained beyond Randidly's reach. Earth itself was still divided into zones and isolated bubble cities, approaching calamities that would test whether it could survive as part of the wider Nexus. Randidly returned home intending to prepare the planet, but without becoming the ruler on whom every institution depended.

Randidly returns to Earth on six months' leave with a rebuilt left arm and the Philosopher's Key. Karen has absorbed thousands of traumatized Chicago arrivals and become prosperous, mobile, and increasingly tied to his image. He orders the city toward Ogre Gorge while encouraging new transport systems built around its intelligent moss spirits. Several threats remain active: a blood-avatar murderer, a distant hostile creature, and the implications of clearing more danger zones. Randidly declines the World Council's Hero Initiative because it resembles surveillance. Helen then delivers James Kelly, leader of the remaining Chicago Chosen. Kelly transfers pain and injury to his followers, so Randidly uses the Philosopher's Key to sever those karmic connections before killing him. The immediate Chosen threat ends, but Randidly is troubled by the moral cost of his action and by Karen's growing dependence on his power.

Karen prepares to leave its isolation while Randidly tests his growing Nether abilities. Earth clears a fourth danger zone, and Nevaeh learns that the next reward would strengthen Earth by absorbing abandoned System structures at a severe cost to displaced populations. The inaugural football game proceeds despite warnings of an attack. Derek Moss, a disillusioned Zone One officer, takes his suspended son Timothy to attend. Randidly identifies guard Ezekiel as the bearer of the next piece of his fate set but refuses to intervene directly in the game, believing Earth must learn to defend itself. Karen crosses Zone One with a conspicuous display of independence and reaches Ogre Gorge. Nearly half a million outside troops are waiting beyond it, so Randidly, Dozer, Sam, and an ogre leader prepare a small delegation to confront their ruler from Randidly's floating island.

Randidly challenges the three-headed ruler of the outside army and wins their duel through superior strength, Nether pressure, and Acri. At the same time, the football game becomes a coordinated Red Revival and Nemesai massacre. Blood seals trap the crowd, controlled attackers infiltrate the stadium, and a ritual begins collecting the victims' blood. Derek prepares explosives, Kirstie attacks the ritual leaders, Nathan protects the wounded, and Ezekiel fights corrupted bodies. Randidly senses the crisis and dispatches a monstrous duplicate of himself from Nordon. Alana enters on Wyvanya, while other defenders contest the invaders and the released creatures. The duplicate breaks into the sealed stadium and confronts the principal blood mage, whose plan depends on mass death and on using the disaster to shape Earth's image of Randidly.

Randidly's intervention kills the principal blood mage and helps end the stadium ritual, but roughly fourteen thousand people die. The disaster discredits one major order, forces the World Council chair to resign, and leaves the lifeblood accumulation in unreliable hands. Randidly admits that he could have prevented more deaths but maintains that Earth must experience the consequences of its own institutional failures. The decision strengthens his Nether as people assign meaning to his restraint, while survivors struggle to understand why their strongest protector did not save everyone. Randidly then comforts his grieving sea soul seed, Thorn, after the death of its closest human companion. Evidence in the dead fighter's home suggests an outside manipulator deliberately distorted the man's grief with promises linked to another Earth. Randidly postpones that confrontation until he is calmer and turns to training.

Derek and Tim choose to relocate to Karen, where Tim's affinity with moss spirits becomes immediately apparent. Derek applies to Karen's order and evolves from Sylvan Archer into Bombardier of the Sacred Grove, a class shaped by his determination to protect his son. Randidly plans a large birthday gathering as a forum for Earth's leaders, System contacts, and even the Nemesai. Before the event, he investigates the ancient woman using Lyra's face, whose Aether has spread through the Endless Sea. He and Nevaeh break through her territorial defenses and descend into a vast engraved cavern. There they confront an aged version of the woman in a chamber she describes as a tomb. Randidly has spent one of his limited liquid-Aether reserves reaching her, while Nevaeh begins dismantling the surrounding arrays.

The woman wearing Lyra's face appears to be dead. She leaves Randidly a canvas that becomes the third piece of his fate set and a recorded account claiming that her husband created the System around their imprisoned pure-energy son. She asks Randidly to free him, but the memory inheritance she intended to leave is missing, apparently taken by people from Earth. Randidly remains suspicious yet accepts that he is grieving an irreplaceable enemy. Nevaeh stays to study the tomb's vast engraving system. Karen prepares for the birthday gathering, and Derek becomes a defense liaison linking city police, ogres, and Randidly's allied forces. Just before the event, Earth clears its fifth danger zone. The reward opens a corrupted-invader scenario requiring hidden bases to be destroyed before the first calamity, with an epic danger zone promised after success.

Randidly learns that the corrupted-invader scenario is nominally a Nexus reward but likely conceals a threat aimed at him. Karen's birthday gathering brings together Earth's rival powers, visitors from beyond cleared danger zones, and the Nemesai. Tim gains the ability to communicate with Karen's moss spirits and joins other children in trying to enter the celebration. The formal image contest exposes deep divisions and competitive ambitions among the guests. Randidly silences the contest and begins the meeting's central purpose: warning the assembled leaders that Earth has only about two weeks to clear the corrupted bases and face the epic zone. His intention is to coordinate action without allowing Earth's factions to mistake their current strength for readiness to survive the Nexus.

The birthday gathering reveals several personal and strategic developments. Ace is secretly combining stolen research, lifeblood essence, and other materials into an egg-like project that he presents as a gift for Randidly. His secrecy and deteriorating behavior make the project potentially dangerous. Ezekiel freely gives Randidly the Dreamcatcher of the Long Night, the second fate-set piece, which allows him to enter dangerous projections of formative memories. The seventh-cohort overseer explains that a powerful family engineered Earth's corrupted-invader trial and placed a Zurt Brigade investigator in position to interfere. Randidly rejects escape to the Nexus and considers confronting the investigator. He then demonstrates his three images before the party, while the overseer's single image overwhelms many of Earth's strongest attendees. After quantifying how poorly comparable worlds survive their calamities, Randidly reveals the four corrupted danger-zone locations and calls on Earth's factions to cooperate.

Earth's factions form four expeditions to attack the corrupted-invader bases. Randidly offers the winged-serpent Nemesai conditional cooperation and refuses Theodora Grayman's request to help give images to Zone One's constrained raid bosses. He begins using the Dreamcatcher to train against Elim and other memories, strengthening Ignition Essence while discovering that Nether use inside the projections can cause real harm. His expanding Nether nebula proves more suppressive than his Aether, though difficult to conceal. Alana negotiates a home for her frost-dragon allies and confirms Hank as her partner. Randidly finally traces the missing memories from the seaside tomb to Ace and Rose. Ace escapes through blood-based movement, but Rose admits that they took the memories. Randidly warns her that any misuse will bring retaliation, leaving the theft as an unresolved personal conflict.

Randidly and Nevaeh flatten his outward Nether into a discreet, self-sustaining field. Karen moves toward diplomat Vi and an approaching bubble-city caravan while its leaders continue expanding political and technical relationships. Lyra reconciles with Randidly and warns that the special investigator intends to attack an Earth task force, but Randidly recognizes that her message was coerced and designed to lure him into a dungeon trap. Seeking enough power to survive the coming confrontation, he uses Dreamcatcher advice to remake the incomplete Ignition Essence. Its inspiration, inherited despair, and phoenix legacy become the Stillborn Phoenix, a concealed black-hole-like pinnacle image with new gravitational and destructive techniques. The investigator's coercion of Lyra and intention to kill Randidly are then confirmed, while the corrupted-invader expeditions continue advancing.

Randidly uses Nevaeh as an illusionary decoy and confronts the special investigator outside the intended dungeon trap. The investigator suspects him of serving Nether but lacks immediate grounds to attack, so both sides prepare for the epic danger zone. Dreamcatcher simulations show Randidly that obvious Nether use lets the investigator isolate him from Earth and drain his strongest advantage. The Swak family also seeks to place another watcher near him. Earth suffers few losses as its task forces advance, and Randidly continues withholding Karen's veterans so local powers develop their own images. When the first corrupted base falls, four Aether Mines activate around Randidly. They inflict escalating pain and threaten to force the detectable Nether response that the investigator needs as evidence. The epic zone's probable location also becomes clear.

The Aether Mines tighten their trap while Randidly searches for a response that will not expose his Nether. Elsewhere, Karen confronts a caravan of nearly half a million people led by the blighted paragon. Diplomat Vi defeats and humiliates the authoritarian leader, dispersing his followers and allowing Karen to absorb selected refugees. Ace takes an interest in the paragon's cellular alteration as material for his rival creation. Randidly turns the mines' punishment into physical training, discovering a spectral barrier that limits what his body can express. With Nevaeh sharing the mental burden and analyzing the devices, he uses non-skill physical force to make the four mines lock together and go inert. The investigator does not immediately react, indicating that the neutralization has escaped notice and giving Randidly two days to pursue his bodily breakthrough.

Randidly tears away the System-imposed limit on his physical body. The System warns that his development contradicts its preferred image-based path and gives him six hours to accept repair or lose its energy, but he refuses. The winged-serpent Nemesai secures neutrality from its people, making future cooperation conditional on Earth's success. Randidly orders Alana and Hank to clear the final corrupted base and places Helen in command of the epic-zone force. Earth completes both operations, gains additional energy and two tier-one Nexus citizenship tokens, and ends the corrupted-invader trial without the investigator appearing. The apparent victory immediately becomes a larger emergency when a dark Great Rift forms above Earth, surrounded by failing Aether structures and carrying a powerful concentration of Nether.

The Great Rift is a Nether rescue offensive aimed at the subdued Nether King supporting the seventh cohort. The special investigator is recalled too late, stripped of rank, and abandoned on Earth, still planning to expose Randidly. Nether probes reach the surface, but Randidly kills their Gatekeeper outside Franksburg while Karen's veterans, Alana, Hank, Helen, and other defenders eliminate the remaining incursions. The Nexus pinnacle figure kills a resistant specialist leader and orders Earth protected. Earth becomes spherical again, gains a new zone, and faces unstable borderlands, bubble cities, and a contest over a runic tablet that can name the planet. Randidly refuses to control that contest or take the available citizenship token. He tells Tatiana that Karen and his organization will withdraw from directing Earth, leaving her and Naffer to build alliances. He plans to enter the Nexus after killing the stranded investigator and recovering Yestix's memories from Ace and Rose.
